conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm inquiring about my son Alex. He's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions.

**Are you responsible for Alex? That is, do you live with him and are you responsible for his day-to-day care and upbringing?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. He's living elsewhere at the moment, but I do help out with some of his costs when I can.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. This is an important detail for Child Benefit eligibility.

**Can you tell me who Alex is currently living with? For example, is he living with another parent, a relative, or is he in care or hospital?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's been in local authority care for a few months now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. When a child is in local authority care, this affects Child Benefit eligibility.

**Can you tell me - do you still contribute to the cost of looking after Alex, or does the local authority cover all of his costs while he's in care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do still contribute when I can. I help out with some money for his things - you know, bits and pieces he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one more important question:

**Is Alex expected to remain in local authority care for more than 8 weeks, or is this a shorter-term arrangement?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's been there for longer than that - about 12 weeks now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I'm afraid **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

Here's why: Under UK Child Benefit rules, when a child is in local authority care for more than 8 weeks, Child Benefit cannot be paid. Since Alex has been in local authority care for approximately 12 weeks, this exceeds the 8-week threshold.

While I understand that you do contribute financially to Alex when you can, the fact that he is in long-term local authority care means Child Benefit is not available during this period.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future - for example, if he comes to live with you again - you may be able to claim Child Benefit at that point. If you have any questions about this decision or need further information, please don't hesitate to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: According to UK Child Benefit rules, when a child is in local authority care for more than 8 weeks, Child Benefit cannot be paid. Alex has been in local authority care for approximately 12 weeks, which exceeds the 8-week threshold. While the claimant does contribute financially to Alex when they can, the fact that Alex is in long-term local authority care and does not live with the claimant means the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex during this period.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is inquiring about Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Alex has been living in local authority care for approximately 12 weeks and does not live with the claimant. Under UK Child Benefit rules, when a child is in local authority care for more than 8 weeks, Child Benefit cannot be paid. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.
